name: batch_design
description:
## Usage

- The batch_design executes a small javascript snippet to modify the document.

- For larger designs, split work into multiple batch_design calls by logical sections (e.g., screen structure first, then sidebar content, then main content).
- Avoid creating large operation objects like an insert with multiple descendants. Prefer breaking it down into many separate operations instead.
- Operations execute sequentially; on error, all operations in the list will be rolled back.
- A list of potential warnings will be returned in the response message. Fix them in the next batch_design call.

- Use normal JavaScript to generate repeated design structure: arrays, `for...of` loops, computed values, conditionals, object spreads, helper functions, and template strings are all useful.
- Be smart about writing JavaScript to remove duplication and minimize the length of the generated code
- Prefer loops/spreads/helpers over long handwritten repetitive code when creating nav items, table rows, cards, metrics, menus, or similar repeated UI.
- Do not include comments in the generated JavaScript snippet. Keep the input small.

- Never set `id` when creating or copying nodes. The ids are automatically generated and returned from the function calls.
- You don't have access to the node properties after they are created.
- Store the returned node IDs in the global scope by not using `const` or `let` to persist them between batch_design tool calls. `const` and `let` variables will not be reachable in the next `batch_design` call.

## Pencil API

Only these pencil functions are supported in batch_design. Use the other MCP tools for other operations.

```
const document: string; // predefined root node

I(parent: string, nodeData: Schema.Child): string; // insert
C(path: string, parent: string, copyNodeData: Schema.Child & { positionPadding?: number; positionDirection?: string }): string; // copy
U(path: string, updateData: Schema.Child): void; // update
R(path: string, nodeData: Schema.Child): string; // replace
M(nodeId: string, parent: string | undefined, index?: number): void; // move
D(nodeId: string): void; // delete
G(nodeId: string, type: "ai" | "stock", prompt: string): void; // generate image
```

### Insert (I)

- Always set `type` in `nodeData`.
- An insert can only be a single node, if you want to add children to it, use the id return value in the next Insert (I) call.
- When working with components (reusable: true), insert their instances as refs with their properties overridden. If you want to override properties of subcomponents use subsequent Update (U) operations.
- Use the Replace (R) to override children inside a component instance, e.g. `R("myInstance/childId",{type:"text",...})`
- Returns the inserted node string ID

### Copy (C)

- "path": The ID of the existing node to copy. If you want to customize some properties of the copied node, just add them next to the `path` property. If you want to customize nested nodes _under_ the copied one, use the same kind of `descendants` map that `ref` nodes use!

- When copying a node and modifying its descendants, you MUST use the "descendants" property in the Copy operation itself. DO NOT use separate Update operations for descendants of copied nodes, as this will fail due to ID mismatches. The copied node and its descendants receive new IDs, so Update operations referencing the original descendant IDs will fail.
- "descendants": Optional, used for components. Keys may be node IDs/paths or unique descendant names inside the referenced component. If a name matches multiple descendants, use the node ID/path instead.
- Example of correct usage: `label1=C("NKYzH",container,{descendants:{"ZopUS/jEYMs":{content:"First Name"}}})`
- Automatically position the copied node by using `positionPadding` and `positionDirection`. Only available in the copy function.
- `positionPadding`: The minimum padding distance from other element when positioning if needed.
- `positionDirection`: The direction to search for empty space relative to the node, to position the copied node if needed. Possible values are: "top", "right", "bottom", "left"

- Copying a reusable node creates a connected instance (a `ref` node).
- Returns the copied node string ID

### Update (U)

- Update the properties of existing nodes, without listing their children.
- DO NOT use this to update the node's `children`, use Replace (R) function for that.
- This function CANNOT change the `id`, `type` or `ref` properties of any node!
- `path`: The valid ID of the existing node to update, or if you want to update a nested node inside a component, use the ID of the nested node must be prefixed with the ID of the component instance and a slash (/). E.g. consider this component:
- path slashes are only valid for nested component instances. Don't use slashes for normal layer structure.
{"id":"button","type":"frame","reusable":true,"children":[{"id":"container","type":"frame","children":[{"id":"label","type":"text","content":"Button text"}]}]}
And then an instance of this component like this: {"id":"submit-button","type":"ref","ref":"button"}
The label text of 'submit-button' can be changed by passing the following as the "nodes" parameter:

This slash-separated instance ID scheme works for any number of nesting levels, not just two.

- `updateData": The node data to update

### Replace (R)

- Replace a node in the .pen file.
- This tool is ideal for swapping out parts of a component instance with new nodes.
- Returns the replacement node's ID as string
- `path`: The path of the node which will be replaced
- `nodeData`: The properties of the new node

### Move (M)

- Move a nodes to a different location in the node tree in a .pen file.
- `path`: The id of the moved node. ALWAYS use a valid node id, NOT a path
- `parent`: Optional parent node Id
- `index`: Optional new position of the moved node among its siblings. If omitted, the node is placed at the end.

### Delete (D)

- Delete a node from a .pen file.
- `nodeId`: The ID of the node to delete. ALWAYS use a valid node id, NOT a path.

### Generate/Get Stock Image (G)

- IMPORTANT: There is NO `image` node type! Images are applied as FILLS to frame/rectangle nodes.
- Do not generate random URLs for image fills, always use the G function to get an image from a stock or AI service.
- To display an image: first Insert a frame or rectangle, then use G to apply the image as a fill.
- `nodeId`: The ID of the node to apply the image fill to.
- `type`: Either "ai" for AI-generated images or "stock" for stock photos from Unsplash.
- `prompt`:
  - For "ai": a detailed descriptive prompt.
  - For "stock": 1-3 keyword search query following Stock query rules (simple, concrete, no use-case or abstract terms).

Examples:
- First create a frame, then apply an ai generated image:

heroImg=I("parentId",{type:"frame",name:"Hero Image",width:400,height:300})
G(heroImg,"ai","modern office workspace bright")

- AI-generated image on existing node:

G("logo-frame","ai","minimalist coffee shop logo, flat design")

### Working with Existing Frames

- Use the frame's ID directly as the parent: `existingFrameId`
- To insert into a slot (a non-unique node with overridden children), use the slot's canonicalized path: `instanceId/slotId`

## Examples

``` javascript
card=I(body,{type:"ref",ref:"CardComp"})
U(card+"/dASd2f",{content:"New Title"})
newTitle=R(card+"/vvd34d",{type:"text",content:"Custom Title"})

container=I(body,{type:"frame",layout:"vertical"})
item=I(container,{type:"text",content:"New item"})

U("MmNEt",{placeholder:true,layout:"horizontal",gap:16})
content=I("MmNEt",{type:"frame",layout:"vertical"})
header=C("A2sa3f",content,{width:"fill_container",height:"fill_container"})

mainContent=I("29c0s",{type:"frame",layout:"vertical",gap:24,padding:32})
stats=I(mainContent,{type:"frame",layout:"vertical",gap:16})
for (const title of ["Revenue","Active users","Conversion"]) {
  const card=I(stats,{type:"ref",ref:"QMBKc",width:"fill_container",height:120})
  U(card+"/fes34s",{content:title})
}
U("FVge3x/gDsgE6S",{content:"Submit"})

input1=I(mainContent,{type:"ref",ref:"FmgD2",width:"fill_container"})
U(input1+"/CvD2R",{content:"First Name"})
contact=R("FmgD2/vfRs3a",{ref:"1YtpE",type:"ref",width:"fill_container"})
U(contact+"/oknii",{content:"Full Name"})

sidebar=I("d3902",{type:"ref",ref:"JRlf7",x:0,y:0,width:240,height:"fill_container(500)"})

dashboardV2=C("Xk9f2",document,{name:"Dashboard V2",positionDirection:"right",positionPadding:100})
D(dashboardV2+"/"+sidebar)
for (const [path,fill] of [["Dfea23s/aSce21","#E8F5E9"],["Dfea23s/aSce22","#FFF3E0"]]) {
  U(dashboardV2+"/"+path,{fill})
}
newContent=R(dashboardV2+"/Dfea23s/aSce23",{type:"text",content:"New Content"})
```

``` javascript
const textBase = {type:"text",fontFamily:"Inter",fontSize:14,fill:"$--font-secondary"};
page=I(document,{type:"frame",name:"Landing Page",layout:"vertical",width:1440,height:"fit_content",placeholder:true});
nav=I(page,{type:"frame",name:"Nav",layout:"horizontal",gap:32,alignItems:"center",width:"fill_container"});

for (const label of ["Menu","Our Story","Visit","Journal"]) {
  I(nav,{...textBase,content:label});
}
U(page,{placeholder:false});
```

For repeated cards, keep shared node properties in an object and spread per-item overrides:

``` javascript
const cardStyle = {type:"frame",layout:"vertical",width:"fill_container",padding:24,gap:8,cornerRadius:16,fill:"$--surface-primary"};
const metrics = [
  ["Orders","1,284","+12"],
  ["Revenue","$48.2K","+8"],
  ["Customers","9,431","+21"]
];
page=I(document,{type:"frame",name:"Dashboard",layout:"vertical",width:1440,height:"fit_content",padding:40,gap:24,placeholder:true});
row=I(page,{type:"frame",layout:"horizontal",gap:16,width:"fill_container"});

for (const [label,value,delta] of metrics) {
  const card = I(row,{...cardStyle,name:`Metric ${label}`});
  const itemStyle = {type:"text",fontSize:13};
  I(card,{...itemStyle,content:label,fill:"$--font-secondary"});
  I(card,{...itemStyle,content:value,fontSize:28,fontWeight:"600",fill:"$--font-primary"});
  I(card,{...itemStyle,content:delta,fill:"$--success"});
}
U(page,{placeholder:false});
```

filePath.description: An optional file path to access a .pen file.

input.description: JavaScript snippet executed in the editor QuickJS environment. Use I/C/R/M/D/U/G operation functions; quote literal node IDs and string values. ONLY properties defined in the .pen schema allowed and their corresponding value format. Using properties not defined in the .pen schema, for exmaple CSS properties will result in failed execution and rollback.
